title: caladenia busselliana
intro: Caladenia busselliana, also known as the Bussell's spider orchid, is a species of orchid native to Western Australia. It is an endangered plant due to habitat loss and degradation. The plant contains bioactive compounds with potential medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Caladenia busselliana are still being researched, but it has been traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures.
